performed_via_github_app.name: GitHub Actions
performed_via_github_app.description: Automate your workflow from idea to production

body:
<!-- greptile_other_comments_section -->

<details><summary>Prompt To Fix All With AI</summary>

`````markdown
This is a comment left during a code review.
Path: frontend/src/scenes/project-homepage/ai-first/aiFirstHomepageLogic.ts
Line: 178

Comment:
**`search` and `hash` dropped on revert**

`router.actions.push` is called with only `pathname`, silently dropping the `search` and `hash` fields from the stored `SceneTab`. Every other place in the codebase that navigates to a `SceneTab` passes all three — see `sceneLogic.tsx` lines 964/980:

```
router.actions.push(tab.pathname, tab.search, tab.hash)
```

For most homepage scenarios (e.g. a plain dashboard URL) this is fine today, but tabs can carry query parameters and hash fragments that would be silently lost.

```suggestion
                router.actions.push(prev.pathname || '/', prev.search || '', prev.hash || '')
```

How can I resolve this? If you propose a fix, please make it concise.
`````

</details>

<sub>Last reviewed commit: ["chore: add a 'revert..."](https://github.com/posthog/posthog/commit/e2680eeadd9939c79edc1e2eb05e51621af8b164)</sub>

performed_via_github_app.name: Greptile Apps
performed_via_github_app.description: Greptile is an AI agent that reviews pull requests with full context of your codebase. It can catch hidden bugs, security vulnerabilities, and anti-patterns.
